notes: Visual phase partially run: dev server unreachable (net::ERR_EMPTY_RESPONSE) during the audit. CDN spot-check for master-hub/screenshot-030-v2.jpg returned 200. Phase E section-label contiguity verified by direct grep on the file (only-04 + 05; no gap). Lightbox markup + script present.

findings.issue: Line 180 uses first-person 'we' twice: 'This quarter, we started validating every partner's data at the gate ...' and 'We also connected 13 PLM masters and 5 mapping tables...'. tone-of-voice §2 Person rule: 'Never "we", "our team", "our people" in user-facing copy. They read as Fynd self-promotion.'
findings.fix: Rewrite to impersonal/institutional voice. e.g. 'This quarter, the platform began validating every partner's data at the gate...' and 'It also connects 13 PLM masters and 5 mapping tables from PLM (Product Lifecycle Management), so information is richer and more complete at the source.'

findings.issue: Hero eyebrow (line 162) reads 'IMPETUS · MASTER HUB · LIVE'. H1 (line 163) reads 'Master Data Platform.' The recent rename told sub-platform pages that 'master-hub is Master Data Platform'. The H1 was renamed; the eyebrow was not. An Apex reviewer reads the two-name conflict in the hero zone first.
findings.fix: Update the eyebrow to 'IMPETUS · MASTER DATA PLATFORM · LIVE' so it matches the H1 and the impetus/index.html card title.

findings.issue: Opener (line 174) 'Think of this as the "single source of truth" for all product and supplier information' uses a conversational/explanatory cadence not in the register. tone-of-voice §1 rule 5: 'Numbers up front. Names up front. Verbs early. No throat-clearing openers.'
findings.fix: Open with the fact: 'Single source of truth for product and supplier data. Validation runs at every ingestion point; downstream systems only receive data that has passed the gate.' (Tagline can move to lede.)

findings.issue: Audience pill #4 (line 166) reads 'Anyone whose downstream work breaks when master data is wrong' — a 12-word colloquial sentence in a pill slot designed for 1-3 word role labels (cf. tone-of-voice §5 Hero subhead pattern, where audience pills carry compact role labels).
findings.fix: Replace with the canonical downstream-consumer roles: 'Procuro · IntelliPack · Cortex · Format Heads' or simply drop and rely on the §02 prose to name them.

findings.issue: 'Every data error caught at the gate is one less fire to fight downstream.' (line 202-203) — the 'fire to fight' metaphor is marketing-deck cadence. Register prefers verifiable verbs.
findings.fix: 'Every data error caught at the gate is one less downstream PO failure or warehouse rejection.' Names the actual failure modes.

findings.issue: Hero pill = 'Live' but the §03 Coverage strip shows '3 partners live (AJIO, LivSmart, Fynd Create)' against a roster of 5 brands listed in the next chip ('Trends · Azorte · Yousta · Shein · AJIO'). The remaining 2 (Shein) and the format wording (Trends, Azorte, Yousta count as a separate entity per the YAML) imply incomplete coverage. 'Live' is defensible (some partners are live) but a finer-grained pill ('Live · partial coverage' or 'Live · 3 of 5 partners') would survive Apex challenge.
findings.fix: Add an inline qualifier next to the Live pill: 'Live · 3 partners of 5 in scope'. Source the count line below the chip strip.

findings.issue: Caption alt texts (line 213, line 220) use the renderer's split-caption artefact: 'Supplier master· 3,334 suppliers...' and 'Upload history· Site Master upload tracking...'. No space before the '·' separator.
findings.fix: Same fix as cortex CTX-4 — sweep build_impetus.py caption split logic.

findings.issue: impetus/index.html line 248 'Plan' band lists platforms as 'NextWave (trends) · Cortex (Command Center) · Master-Hub (CMT) · Analytics (SuperSet)'. The card headline at line 166 uses 'Master Data Platform' but the value-chain band reverts to 'Master-Hub (CMT)'. Two names for the same surface circulate on the same parent page.
findings.fix: Update the value-chain band on impetus/index.html:248 to 'Master Data Platform (CMT)' or just 'Master Data Platform' to match the card title and the page H1.

strengths: Section labels are contiguous (hero · 02 · 03 · 04 · 05) — no gap from the deleted §05 Updates Shipped + Sources sections.
§04 What improved is name-naming and concrete: 'Procuro ingestion failures', 'IntelliPack rejections', 'Specialist buyers (ODM)', 'Format teams' — every claim points at a real downstream consumer.
Screenshot URLs use the -v2/-v2-b versioned pattern; both load (CDN spot-check 200); lightbox markup + script present.
Eyebrow + status-pill + chip strip carry no Accenture attributions or stripped-source residue (the Accenture-stripping pass landed cleanly).
Cortex eyebrow + sub-section label disambiguate it from Cortex (Planning Engine) cleanly — no mega-menu drift on Master Data Platform on the cortex page or vice versa.
